sanskrit:
अनिष्टमिष्टं मिश्रं च त्रिविधं कर्मणः फलम्।
भवत्यत्यागिनां प्रेत्य न तु संन्यासिनां क्वचित्॥
transliteration:
aniṣṭam iṣṭaṃ miśraṃ ca tri-vidhaṃ karmaṇaḥ phalam |
bhavaty atyāgināṃ pretya na tu sannyāsināṃ kvacit ||
literal_meaning: Undesirable, desirable, and mixed—these three kinds of results of action accrue after death to the non-renouncer, but never to the sannyāsī.
contextual_meaning: The mind that clings to outcomes continues to harvest them; the one that has psychologically ‘dropped’ the claim to results is no longer haunted by reward or regret.
psychological_state: Cognitive dis-identification from the fruits of action; ego no longer contracts around success/failure, producing a post-traumatic-growth-like freedom.
trigger_scenario: You check your investment app, see a crash, and feel your chest tighten—then remember you moved the money to an index fund you promised not to touch for 30 years; the tightening dissolves.
anchor_text: Drop the claim to results and walk free of regret or reward.
